perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall include any financial, business, proprietary or technical information of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall include any financial, business, proprietary or technical Data of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “information” to “Data” in the definition of Confidential Information can create a contradiction. The agreement uses “Confidential Information” extensively, while “Data” is now subtly introduced, it creates confusion whether it implies 'Confidential Information' and creates conflict when interpreting the obligations and rights related to both terms. It's unclear whether the provisions applicable to 'Confidential Information' automatically apply to 'Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All such Confidential Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose and neither Party shall disclose or otherwise use th e Confidential Information for any other purpose or in any other manner without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from "information" to "Data" in the definition of Confidential Information introduces a new term, 'Data,' that is not consistently used throughout the document. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information,' and it is unclear whether 'Data' is intended to be included within this term. This misalignment creates ambiguity and potential conflict regarding the obligations related to 'Confidential Information,' as it's uncertain if they automatically apply to 'Data'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All such Confidential Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose and neither Party shall disclose or otherwise use th e Confidential Information for any other purpose or in any other manner without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All such Sensitive Information shared under this Agreement shall be used by the Parties exclusively for the Purpose and neither Party shall disclose or otherwise use th e Sensitive Information for any other purpose or in any other manner without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Sensitive Information” creates a contradiction. The agreement uses “Confidential Information” to describe the information being disclosed, it creates confusion whether it implies 'Sensitive Information' and creates conflict when interpreting the obligations and rights related to both terms. It's unclear whether the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1 applies to 'Sensitive Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information ” shall mean and include all non -public information, written or oral, disclosed, directly or indirectly, through any means of communication or observation (including oral, graphic, written or electronic form) by the Disclosing Party or any of its affiliates or representatives to or for the benefit of the Receiving Party from the Effective Date, irrespective of whether such inform ation: (a) has been specifically marked as “confidential” at the time of disclosure; (b) is treated as proprietary information by the Disclosing Party; or (c) is owned or developed by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Sensitive Information" creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text defines "Confidential Information." By using "Sensitive Information" in the changed text instead, it's unclear if the definition of "Confidential Information" applies to "Sensitive Information," leading to confusion about the obligations and rights related to the disclosed information. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shared under this Agreement may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to other employees on a n eed to know basis, with written consent from the Disclosing Party, in connection with the Purpose, and who shall protect the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Data shared under this Agreement may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to other employees on a n eed to know basis, with written consent from the Disclosing Party, in connection with the Purpose, and who shall protect the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party has to protect the Confidential Information as the text states it and there is no indication of the "Confidential Data", or if the data is also information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Confidential Inform ation in the same manner as it would protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly states that "The Receiving Party shall protect the Confidential Information". The changed text introduces a new term, "Confidential Data", while still requiring the Receiving Party to protect "Confidential Information". The change makes it unclear whether "Confidential Data" is the same as "Confidential Information" or whether both must be protected separately, creating ambiguity and a potential conflict in obligations. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees not to issue or release for publication any arti cles or advertising or publicity matter relating to this Agreement which mention or imply the name of the Disclosing Party any of its affiliates, or subject matter hereof, unless prior written consent is granted by the Disclosing Party subject only to Clau se 7.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client agrees not to issue or release for publication any arti cles or advertising or publicity matter relating to this Agreement which mention or imply the name of the Disclosing Party any of its affiliates, or subject matter hereof, unless prior written consent is granted by the Disclosing Party subject only to Clau se 7.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Receiving Party” to “Client” in this context creates a contradiction because the agreement states in Section 5 about the "Receiving Party" without taking into account that can also be a "Client".
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall protect the Confidential Inform ation in the same manner as it would protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Receiving Party" to "Client" introduces a contradiction because the contract refers to both terms without clarifying that they can be the same entity, especially in Section 5. The explanation explicitly states this creates a contradiction because the agreement states in Section 5 about the 'Receiving Party' without taking into account that can also be a 'Client'. The contradicted text uses 'Receiving Party,' implying a specific entity. If 'Client' and 'Receiving Party' are meant to be interchangeable, it isn't defined, which leads to ambiguity and potential conflict in enforcement. Therefore, the change introduces a misaligned terminology, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the req uest of the Disclosing Party or upon the termination of this Agreement, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including drawings, documents, reports and other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received by the Receiv ing Party pursuant to this Agreement, together with all copies and reproductions thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client shall, upon the req uest of the Disclosing Party or upon the termination of this Agreement, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including drawings, documents, reports and other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received by the Receiv ing Party pursuant to this Agreement, together with all copies and reproductions thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Receiving Party” to “Client” in this context creates a contradiction because the agreement states that the "Confidential Information" has to be received by the "Receiving Party", and it's the "Client" who is returing such documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Client shall, upon the req uest of the Disclosing Party or upon the termination of this Agreement, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, including drawings, documents, reports and other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received by the Receiv ing Party pursuant to this Agreement, together with all copies and reproductions thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Receiving Party" with "Client." The Contradicted Text states that the "Confidential Information" has to be received by the "Receiving Party", but the Changed Text states that it is the "Client" who returns the documents. This creates a contradiction because the Client cannot return something they didn't receive, based on the contract's terminology. This creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definitions.
